zsh-users/zsh-syntax-highlighting.author: Daniel Shahaf
zsh-users/zsh-syntax-highlighting.message: dev tools: Print the test data to stdout for convenience.

zsh-users/zsh-syntax-highlighting.author: Matthew Martin
zsh-users/zsh-syntax-highlighting.message: tests: Use root variable for root of the project
removed_lines: -  run_test "$data_file" | tee >($results_filter | $root/tests/tap-colorizer.zsh) | grep -v '^not ok.*# TODO' | grep -Eq '^not ok|^ok.*# TODO' && (( something_failed=1 ))
added_lines: +  run_test "$data_file" | tee >($results_filter | ${0:A:h}/tap-colorizer.zsh) | grep -v '^not ok.*# TODO' | grep -Eq '^not ok|^ok.*# TODO' && (( something_failed=1 ))

zsh-users/zsh-syntax-highlighting.author: Daniel Shahaf
zsh-users/zsh-syntax-highlighting.message: dev tools: Stage the copyright block to make diffs smaller.

zsh-users/zsh-syntax-highlighting.author: Matthew Martin
zsh-users/zsh-syntax-highlighting.message:
tests: Fix grep syntax

In basic regular expressions | is not a special character that spearates
branches, so use an extended regular expression.
removed_lines: -  run_test "$data_file" | tee >($results_filter | ${0:A:h}/tap-colorizer.zsh) | grep -v '^not ok.*# TODO' | grep -Eq '^not ok|^ok.*# TODO' && (( something_failed=1 ))
added_lines: +  run_test "$data_file" | tee >($results_filter | ${0:A:h}/tap-colorizer.zsh) | grep -v '^not ok.*# TODO' | grep -q '^not ok\|^ok.*# TODO' && (( something_failed=1 ))

zsh-users/zsh-syntax-highlighting.author: Daniel Shahaf
zsh-users/zsh-syntax-highlighting.message:
tests: Add a 'print failures only' mode to 'make test', called 'make quiet-test'.

Fixes zsh-users/zsh-syntax-highlighting#262.

Currently, 'make quiet-test' uses Perl.  However, since it is considered a development
tool rather than a user-facing tool, users and downstream packages needn't install Perl.
Furthermore, even this dev-only dependency may be dropped in the future.

The only difference between tests/tap-filter here and the one in the issue is using
a `cat` subshell v. using 'undef $/; <STDIN>'.
removed_lines: -  run_test "$data_file" | tee >($results_filter | ${0:A:h}/tap-colorizer.zsh) | grep -v '^not ok.*# TODO' | grep -q '^not ok\|^ok.*# TODO' && (( something_failed=1 ))
added_lines: +  run_test "$data_file" | tee >(${0:A:h}/tap-colorizer.zsh) | grep -v '^not ok.*# TODO' | grep -q '^not ok\|^ok.*# TODO' && (( something_failed=1 ))

zsh-users/zsh-syntax-highlighting.author: Daniel Shahaf
zsh-users/zsh-syntax-highlighting.message:
tests: Tweak the isolation wrapper for style and robustness.

Followup to c01533920245599e4fbc4a1825520737b2c1a6ce.

Suggested-by: Matthew Martin
removed_lines: -  run_test "$data_file" | tee >(${0:A:h}/tap-colorizer.zsh) | grep -v '^not ok.*# TODO' | grep -q '^not ok\|^ok.*# TODO' && (( something_failed=1 ))
added_lines: +  (run_test "$data_file") | tee >(${0:A:h}/tap-colorizer.zsh) | grep -v '^not ok.*# TODO' | grep -q '^not ok\|^ok.*# TODO' && (( something_failed=1 ))

zsh-users/zsh-syntax-highlighting.author: Daniel Shahaf
zsh-users/zsh-syntax-highlighting.message:
'make test': Fix breakage introduced by 4d3da30f8b726c6e0eeb3adda06adc9eaa329b3b:
test failure would not be reflected by the exit code of 'make'.

Setting a shell parameter in the left-hand side of a pipe is not visible to
commands after the pipe, because the left-hand side forks.  (That's true both
in 'sh' used by 'make' and in 'zsh' that runs tests/test-highlighting.zsh, at
least on my system.)  Therefore, move the colorizing hook to where it doesn't
interfere with setting the $something_failed (in tests/test-highlighting.zsh)
and $result (in Makefile) parameters.
removed_lines: -  (run_test "$data_file") | tee >(${0:A:h}/tap-colorizer.zsh) | grep -v '^not ok.*# TODO' | grep -q '^not ok\|^ok.*# TODO' && (( something_failed=1 ))
added_lines: +  (run_test "$data_file") | tee >(cat) | grep -v '^not ok.*# TODO' | grep -q '^not ok\|^ok.*# TODO' && (( something_failed=1 ))

zsh-users/zsh-syntax-highlighting.author: Daniel Shahaf
zsh-users/zsh-syntax-highlighting.message: test harness: Exit 1 if a 'todo' test passed.
removed_lines: -  (run_test "$data_file") | tee >(cat) | grep -v '^not ok.*# TODO' | grep -q '^not ok\|^ok.*# TODO' && (( something_failed=1 ))
added_lines: +  (run_test "$data_file") | tee >(cat) | grep '^not ok' | grep -qv ' # TODO' && (( something_failed=1 ))

zsh-users/zsh-syntax-highlighting.author: Julien Nicoulaud
zsh-users/zsh-syntax-highlighting.message:
Add a mechanism for testing the correctness of the highlighting.
Feel free to add more test data !

Signed-off-by: Julien Nicoulaud <julien.nicoulaud@gmail.com>
removed_lines: -BUFFER='ps aux | grep java | sort | uniq | tail | head'
added_lines: +ps aux | grep java | sort | uniq | tail | head
